title: Telling Forest Stories with Tableau
text:
Exploring Data Storytelling, I chose to work with data from Madagascar—the country where I currently live. One of the most alarming environmental issues here is deforestation, which is not just a matter of numbers but a threat to biodiversity, communities, and the future of the island.
As part of a Tableau course (available at Coursera, there is a lot of options there actually…), I built interactive dashboards to visualize tree cover loss across time and regions.

The goal: raise awareness of Madagascar’s dramatic forest loss.
This project showed me how data visualization can tell stories that move beyond statistics—inviting reflection, and hopefully, inspiring action.

title: Wheat, R and love for data
text:
During my master’s thesis and my first job in Europe, I worked with one of its most iconic staple foods: wheat. My years in wheat research gave me much more than scientific results—they gave me R.
The mission: analyze agro-climatic, sensor-based data and genetic data to understand plant resilience and improve grain yield.

These projects taught me that data is never abstract—it is the key to rethinking and adapting our food systems in the face of climate change.
📄 I’ve published some scientific articles that you can explore here:
Frontiers paper

title: Stats training with APDRA
text:
We’re wrapping up the year with a project that was both challenging, motivating, and truly rewarding. My first training as the coach (hopefully, the first one from many others to come).
It was a statistics training called Stats sans stress, because statistics are not meant to stress us out — they’re meant to help us make sense of data.
The training took place in Antsirabe, Madagascar, with technical evaluation officers from APDRA (Association Pisciculture et Développement Rural in Africa).
We worked on data management and cleaning, as well as the foundations of essential statistical tests, always tailored to the group’s real needs. We explored lots of fish-related data 🐟 and dove into this fascinating world to turn numbers into clear, meaningful stories backed by solid statistics.
Because data do tell stories — and learning how to read them can be both useful and fun.
If you’d like to learn how to work with data in a clear, useful, and stress-free way with your team, feel free to get in touch.

title: Hello and welcome!
text:
Making data



Fun




Clear




Easy



for all your projects!



Hey, I’m so glad you’re here! 🌸
Welcome to my personal website — a little space where I share my love for data, creativity, and teaching.
Here you’ll find content about R, Python, data science, and other things I explore along the way.
If you’re curious about how I can help you, here’s what I offer:




📊 Data analysis



Data analysis & visualization: let’s unlock the value of your data with statistics, graphs, and insights, always aiming for results that are reproducible and automated.







🎓 Trainings



Workshops and trainings in R, Python, or data management, designed to help you optimize your analyses and grow your skills with confidence.







💡 Tips & Blog



On my blog, I share resources, tutorials, and reflections.It’s my way of passing along what has helped me, hoping it inspires you too.





Feel free to look around, discover my work, and contact me anytime — I’d be happy to connect.
“Making complex data simple, useful, and reliable for everyone”

title: About me
text:
Hi, I’m Ana, a Mexican currently at Madagascar —for now—. I’m a woman of many passions, led by creativity, logic, and the joy of transformation.
Here I share some of them, so you can get to know me better.

Data analysisBio & techContinous LearningHobbies


Curiosity didn’t kill the cat—for me, exploring is a necessity. I love seeing how digging into data can turn into knowledge, clearer decisions, and better understanding.
My expertise lies in programming, statistics, and data reproducibility, which I’ve practiced across international projects, from genomics to image analysis. I’ve developed skills in multiple programming languages (R, Python, SQL) and visualization tools (Tableau, Shiny, Quarto) and I love data challenges.


With a degree in Biotechnology and a Master’s in Plant Breeding, my most recent projects have been linked to agriculture and, more broadly, to innovation and technology.
Advances in tech can be both inspiring and risky, which is why I enjoy staying informed, learning how to make better use of technologies, reflecting on ethics, and exploring how we can move forward together.


I love learning—both in science and in life. From languages (I speak Spanish, English, French, Italian, and now a bit of Malagasy) to geography.
I also love exploring new cultures and places, that’s what brought me to Mada in the 1st place.


My hobbies include dancing, painting, sports, and reading. I enjoy challenging myself with new things, right now it’s volleyball and diving.




Let’s connect!

title: From Pixels to Guavas
text:
Freelance projects sometimes push you into new worlds—mine brought me to computer vision.
With a fruit producer company in Mexico, I have been working on building an image analysis pipeline in Python for a guava breeding program.

The challenge: create an automated system to measure, analyze, and classify different guava traits.

Along the way, I discovered tools I’d never heard of before as PlantCV (open-source image analysis software package, specifically for plant science) and I have been increasing my abilities in Python.
It’s been a winding road, but one that taught me how versatile data can be when combined with image analysis and -of course - creativity.

title: R training at dP SPAD
text:
Stats without stress? Oh yes, it’s a match… and we are still going!
From April 13th to 17th, the dP SPAD (Research and Training Platform in Partnership “High-Altitude Production Systems and Sustainability”) in Ambatobe, Madagascar, hosted an intensive week on R, RStudio, and data analysis. But wait, we didn’t stop there!
This wasn’t just a one-week workshop; it is an ongoing learning journey. One month later, the adventure continues with 10 brilliant PhD students from dP SPAD, CIRAD (French Agricultural Research Centre for International Development), and their partners.
Through online follow-ups and personalized 1-on-1 mentoring, we are breaking down every function and fine-tuning every script to fit their exact research data.
Because let’s be honest: programming in R and statistics shouldn’t be so stressful. They are here to make your data speak beautifully!
Kudos to these amazing researchers for their hard work and dedication.
Want to transform how your team works with data? If you’d like to learn how to work with data in a clear, useful, and stress-free way with your team, feel free to get in touch.
